content: Well, last weekend was kind of tough, honestly. When they invited me to that lunch, I just felt this wave of anxiety—like everyone seemed so familiar with what I had to say. I kept smiling because I wanted them to think I was smart, but then I found myself agreeing with whatever they said, even if I didn’t really believe it myself. Laughing when things weren’t funny made me feel less important, and laughing too much sometimes just seemed like a way to try and seem okay. It’s exhausting trying to fit in somehow without making myself look ridiculous or bothering anyone. I guess part of me worries that if I show any weakness, people might just see through me. It’s hard to shake off the sense that maybe I’m just not cut out for these kinds of relationships. I’d appreciate it if you could just understand how much I’m struggling. Thank you for being such an important patient to me. I need someone to talk to about this. I just want to get away from this whole thing. Thanks!

Yeah, this past week has been pretty rough. My husband seems much more interested in his work than usual, which makes me feel like maybe we’re just not cut out for each other. I keep thinking, “Why does he even bother talking when he doesn’t seem to care?” It’s hard to explain to myself how I’m failing him somehow. I guess I just feel... invisible, like I’m always missing out. Sometimes I wonder if I’m just not good enough for him anymore. It’s exhausting trying to stop what feels like an impossible tide. I don’t know, I just feel like I need to push harder or maybe make things better before I lose him completely. It’s scary to think about what might come next. I’m scared that if I let go too soon, I’ll just end up feeling worse off again. It’s frustrating because I want to connect, but then I just shut down. I’ve tried to keep track of our progress, but it feels like no matter what I do, I’m still falling further behind. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I feel like I’m already doomed before I even start. I wish I could tell someone else about how I feel, but I don’t know where to start. It’s like I’m afraid of losing all the proof that I’m already failing. So, I just keep hoping that maybe he’s starting to see why I’m different. But honestly, I don’t believe there’s anything wrong with me—there’s always something going wrong anyway. I suppose it’s just easier to pretend everything’s okay than face the pain of losing trust altogether. It’s hard to shake off the nagging feeling that I’m not meant to be here. I appreciate you asking about this, but I’m sorry if my responses feel fake. I really wanted to share how I feel, but I just feel stuck.

Yeah, I guess... when they gave me that big project, I just felt like maybe I’m not cut out for this. Like, no matter how much time I’ve spent working on stuff before, I still find myself falling behind. I keep thinking, “What if everyone sees I can’t handle this?” And honestly, once I start putting off trying, I feel kind of stuck because there’s just so much pressure trying to get everything right. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know how to stop worrying about it. Sometimes I just want to disappear into a corner and not deal with it at all. I mean, I know I should probably push through, but I’m scared of failing. It’s like, if people discover I’m not really competent enough, then they’ll actually see me as a waste. So, I end up staying put and avoiding talking to my coworkers about it. It’s frustrating because I want to prove I can do it, but I just can’t seem to keep up. I’m sorry if I sound like I’m complaining, but it just feels too much to bear. I appreciate you understanding, but I’m hoping you can help me figure out what’s going on. Maybe we can talk about it in a way that makes sense? Or maybe you think it’s better if I just let things be? Thanks!

Well, this past weekend was really tough for me. I tried to join the party, but honestly, every time I saw someone whispering, I just felt like I was being watched. I kept thinking maybe they were just talking about how awkward I am, or maybe I sounded weird or silly. So, I ended up smiling too much and agreeing with everyone because I didn’t want to seem like I wasn’t going to be part of it. It’s exhausting being caught up in that kind of fearfulness. I mostly stayed away because I didn’t feel like participating or connecting much anyway. It just made me feel worse about myself and harder to connect with people outside of my family. I guess I’m scared that if I show my true feelings or disagree, they’ll just see me as a complete failure. It’s frustrating because I want to contribute, but then I just end up feeling like I’m failing again. I wish there was some way to stop feeling so anxious and isolated at the party. Can you give me any advice on how to navigate these kinds of situations better? Or maybe you could explain things in a way that makes me feel more secure about not being able to participate? I’d love to hear from someone who understands how these feelings and thoughts might manifest. Thanks!

Yeah, it was really hard to respond at first. I kept thinking, “Why even try? They’re already doing things differently anyway,” like always, and then suddenly I just shut down. When the topic changed to something else, I kind of gave up and said, “Okay, whatever.” It felt like no matter what I tried, it didn’t work out, and I just ended up feeling more trapped inside the relationship than ever before. I don’t know, it’s like I’m stuck in this cycle where I can’t escape whatever’s happening in my life or change it. It’s exhausting to keep pretending everything’s okay, but honestly, I’m scared that if I show any sign of defiance or resistance, I’ll just prove how incapable I am. I guess I just don’t feel like I belong in this particular relationship anymore. It’s frustrating because I want to push back and be seen as important, but I’m afraid of disappointing anyone or themselves. So, I just stick to what feels safe and let things take their course. It’s like I’m trapped in a never-ending loop where nothing changes and I feel worse about myself. I wish there was a way to break free, but I’m too attached to this relationship to think otherwise. It’s exhausting to admit that I need help, but I just don’t feel ready to face it yet. I’m sorry I’ve been this way, but it’s hard to see why I can’t get out of this mess. Please, help me figure out how to start moving forward. It’s like I’m waiting for someone to pull the trigger, but I’m scared it won’t come. I appreciate you understanding how much this situation means to me, but I really need you to see through my thoughts. Thank you for being here. It’s been tough, but I appreciate you being here. I really need you to know how

Yeah, this past weekend was really tough for me at the family reunion. When they started talking about different careers and lifestyles that didn’t fit what I wanted, I just felt this overwhelming pressure to stick to what everyone thinks. I kept thinking, “Why even try? What if it doesn’t work out?” And honestly, when I tried to say something, it just shut down or made me sound dumb. I ended up just sitting quietly most of the time, which wasn’t very satisfying, but I guess I didn’t want to make a scene or cause any problems. It’s like every time someone says something different, I feel like I’m stuck on the same path, and then it feels like everything’s gone wrong. I guess part of me feels embarrassed because I know I’m going against my own desires, but I can’t shake the fear that I’ll never escape this kind of pattern. It’s exhausting trying to keep up with all that pressure without getting nowhere. I’ve been having trouble connecting with friends lately, so maybe this whole thing is just starting to get old. Just knowing I need to figure this out before it’s too late makes me feel pretty lost and scared. I’m sorry if this feels like I’m not answering them right, but I just don’t feel ready to let go yet. I’d prefer to stay away from these kinds of conversations altogether. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how else to handle this situation. Please help me connect with the therapist about this situation. I appreciate you understanding where I’m coming from. I’m looking forward to our next meeting. Thank you for being here and giving me space to express myself freely. I truly appreciate you being here. I love you, and I hope we can talk some good stuff soon. I’ll miss you.

I don’t know, it just feels like everything’s spiraling out of control because they’re trying to push me to do something wrong. I keep thinking, “What if I fail? What if I mess up,” and then I just shut down and pretend nothing matters. It’s exhausting, really. Honestly, it makes me feel completely useless, like I’m just contributing less to what they want rather than what they need. I guess I’m scared that if I say too much or show some independence, people will just leave or say I’m not cut out for this kind of life. It’s frustrating because I want to grow and express myself, but I just feel like I’m stuck in this cycle of being pushed away. I’ve tried to talk to them about it, but no one seems interested or wants to listen. It’s like I’m invisible or maybe even an obstacle in their plan. I wish I could break free somehow, but every time I try, it just feels harder and worse off. It’s hard enough without it feeling like I’m constantly being pushed around. I appreciate you seeing through this, but it’s sinking in how much I’m already being held back. I’m sorry I can’t help solve this problem. Please, if you ever get the chance, I’d love to talk about this with someone who understands how much it’s holding me back. I just need someone to tell me I’m right and that I can actually change things. Thank you for being here. It’s been tough, but knowing that I’m not alone helps me keep pushing forward.

Well, um, during that argument, my partner mentioned that sometimes I don’t express myself because I feel like maybe they’ll get annoyed or even disapprove. I just kind of shut down and agreed with everything he said, even when I felt like it was irrelevant or wrong. It’s like if I say something different, it might hurt his feelings or make me seem like I’m trying too hard. I guess I feel pretty lost and anxious most of the time around our arguments. It’s easier to just follow along than face the pain of being rejected. I wish I could tell him why I don’t want to talk anymore. It’s like I’m afraid of being discovered or judged. So, I mostly stay quiet because if I voice my opinion, it might just prove I’m wrong or that I’ve messed up somehow. It’s exhausting being stuck in this loop where I just don’t know what to say. I appreciate you seeing through this, though. It makes me realize that controlling things seems futile. Thanks for telling me about it, and I’ll try harder next time. It’s tough, but at least knowing how to handle it helps. Reducing my defenses feels safer.

I’ve been feeling like everyone’s just ignoring me at this family event. It’s like I’m invisible even when I’m there. I keep thinking maybe they’re not interested in me or maybe they don’t really care about me anyway. It’s exhausting trying to join in, but then I end up feeling even worse off than before. I guess part of me feels like I’m just… not good enough, and that makes me feel even more vulnerable. It’s hard to connect with people if I seem distant or less interesting. I don’t know why I keep going back to that room; it just feels safer to stay out of the mix. It’s frustrating because I want to fit in and be seen as important, but I’m just not getting anywhere. I’m sorry I can’t tell you how many times I’ve felt this way lately. Please help me figure out what’s happening. I appreciate you knowing how hard this is for me. I just need someone to understand where I’m headed. Thanks!

Yeah, I looked at those bills again this morning, and honestly, it just made me feel worse. I don’t know, sometimes when I’m struggling with money, I just feel like I’m failing at something. It’s like no matter how hard I work, I can’t even get ahead of the bills. I keep thinking maybe I’ve done something wrong or let myself down because of all these numbers. It’s exhausting being trapped in this cycle where I just want to stop and figure out how to fix things better. I guess I’m scared of showing anyone I’m struggling or admitting I can’t handle everything. It’s hard to shake off that feeling of shame and hopelessness. I wish I could find a way to move forward, but instead, I just end up dwelling on it and feeling like I’m stuck somewhere. It’s frustrating because I want to make some kind of change, but I’m just stuck in this never-ending loop of feeling inadequate. I’ll probably just end up staying put and avoiding any new opportunities. It’s scary to think about what might come next. So, it’s easier to just stay quiet and pretend nothing’s happening. But then again, if I keep pushing myself further than necessary, maybe I’ll prove I’m capable of handling things properly. Maybe it’s better to just accept that I can’t fix everything right now. It’s tough, but at least I know there’s a limit to how much pressure I can take on alone. I’m sorry if this seems like I’m coming across like XXX. I didn’t mean to make this too personal, but it just felt relevant. Thank you for joining me in this conversation about these feelings. I appreciate you being here and sharing your perspective. It helps me understand where I’m at. Please let me know if there’s anything else I can help clarify or answer. I’ll be happy to explain how I

I didn’t really expect to get this chance, but I guess sometimes things just go downhill like they always do. When I see how many people are talking about the promotion, I feel like I’m just not cut out for this job. I keep thinking, “What if I mess something up? What if everyone sees how stupid I am?” It’s scary because I don’t want to show anyone why I think I can’t handle this. Honestly, I just shut down and try to pretend everything’s okay, even though inside I’m really scared of failing. Sometimes, being too bold makes me feel worse, so I just stay put and avoid any discussion about it. It’s like I’m trapped between wanting to prove myself and fearing public scrutiny. I don’t know why I keep putting off applying, but I just feel like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m trying to prove myself when I’m actually struggling. It’s exhausting knowing I’m not ready for this kind of responsibility. I wish I could tell them what I’m afraid of, but I don’t want to seem like I’m giving them proof of my worthlessness. So, I’ve just kept quiet and pushed back, hoping maybe they’ll let me in. It’s frustrating because I feel like I’m falling further behind every time I push harder. I’m sorry if this seems grating, but I just feel like I need to protect myself from losing control.

Yeah, it’s just been really overwhelming lately. When they gave me that big project, like, right after work, I felt this whole weight lifting off me, but then I started thinking, “What’s going on here? Can’t I handle something so massive?” I kept putting it off because honestly, I didn’t want to seem incompetent or let everyone down. After that, I just stayed home late most nights and avoided talking to anyone because I just felt trapped or like I was failing somehow. It’s like no matter what I try, it always fails me in some way. I guess part of me just wants to say, “I’m sorry, I don’t know how to handle this,” but I’m afraid that’ll just prove how inadequate I really am. It’s exhausting trying to get through it without getting completely wrecked. So, I end up staying behind and avoiding anything that seems complicated or risky. Sometimes I wonder why I even bother trying to plan things properly when it feels so futile. I feel pretty useless, kind of stuck in a never-ending loop of fear. It’s frustrating because I want to tackle this better, but every time I push myself further, it just feels harder to pull back. I appreciate when I just have to admit I can’t fix this alone. It’s hard to connect with someone who seems convinced they can’t solve everything. So, I guess I’m scared of disappointing them or proving to them that I’m not good enough. It’s exhausting to navigate through this pressure and still feel like I’m just not cut out to handle things. I’d love to talk about it, but I’m not sure where to start. Can you give me some ideas on how to approach that project differently? Or maybe just tell me if there’s a particular step I should skip? I’d really appreciate it if you could just lend me a hand in this. I

Yeah, the meeting last week was kind of rough for me. When I first met these new people, I just felt like I was there to be noticed, not really anyone wanted to spend time with me. I kept thinking, “Why bother being around them if no one really seems interested?” It’s like everyone was already talking about me, and I wasn’t really contributing anything unique or interesting. I ended up just staying quiet most of the time, nodding along, but barely saying anything because I didn’t want to seem like I was bothering anyone. After leaving, I just felt completely exhausted and kind of defeated inside. It’s like the more I tried to connect, the harder it got to feel like I’m being watched or judged. I guess part of me believes that if I keep trying to make people happy all the time, they’ll eventually stop loving me. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I don’t know how to stop feeling so defeated. Maybe I need to step back and focus more on myself instead of just pretending to be someone else. But I’m afraid I’m failing if I don’t stick to being someone who makes people laugh and say things they might actually believe. It’s frustrating because I want to connect, yet it feels like it’s never coming out. I’d appreciate it if you could give me some advice on how to start connecting better. Thank you!

Yeah, I guess I just felt like maybe I’m always putting the other person first when I’m at home. They’re probably thinking I’m annoying or something, like I’m taking too many responsibilities or just... not being there right. So, when they start complaining about how much I’ve been spending time alone, I just kind of shut down and try to fix things. But then, after apologizing and canceling some things, I feel better for a little while, but inside I’m still feeling really overwhelmed and sad. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m just proving that I’m not good enough. Honestly, sometimes I wonder if they actually find out I’m being too much. I know it’s not fair, but it’s hard to shake off these recurring thoughts. It’s like I’m always scared that if I say anything, they’ll see how useless I am. I appreciate you knowing me, though. Maybe we can talk through this a bit more? I’d love to hear more about what’s going on in my life than just repeat the same old stuff.

I don’t know, I just feel like right now, I’m drowning inside. Every time I get up to speak, it feels like everyone’s staring at me with those big eyes that say, “You’re not cut out for this.” It’s like I’m invisible until I present, and then suddenly I’m the one everyone notices. I keep thinking, maybe I’m not even good enough to handle this. It makes me want to just say, “No,” but then I end up playing around all day, making so much silly preparation that I’m exhausted and already feeling worse about myself. Honestly, it’s hard to believe I’ve been stuck here for this long. I guess I just don’t trust myself anymore. It’s like I’m scared of failing again and leaving behind what I’ve built. So, I ended up saying no, and now I’m stuck backtracking and worrying about everything. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know how else to stop feeling this way. I’ll probably go ahead anyway, but knowing that I’m going to disappoint just doesn’t make me want to change my path. I wish there was some way to fix this before I have to lose everything. It feels like the sooner I admit this, the easier it will be to move forward. Please, if you can, help me figure out why I’m so stuck. I really want to get out of this feeling, but I don’t know how to stop. I’m sorry, I’m just not ready for this yet.

Well, um, yeah, during the dinner, my partner suggested going to this restaurant, and then I said no. I figured maybe they’d notice if I had some idea or wanted something different. But inside, I felt really butterflies in my tummy and kind of sad that I ended up agreeing anyway. Honestly, I just kept thinking, “Maybe they’re right, maybe I’m not interesting enough.” It’s hard not to feel like they’re judging me or not liking me. I guess I’m scared that if I tried to say what I really want, they might start ignoring me or not like me. So, I just shut down and agree because it feels safer than risk. It’s like I don’t want to upset them or cause any tension. I keep wondering if there’s anything wrong with me if I’ve let myself get so caught up in things. It’s frustrating because I want to be honest and open, but sometimes I just freeze up. What would you say instead? I guess I could say something along the lines of, “I appreciate the plan, but I’m not ready to go yet. Can we talk later about something else?” That way, at least I can say something positive. Or maybe just ask if they were interested in trying another option. I’m not sure what to say exactly, but I’m worried if I tell them how I really feel, they’ll think less of me. It’s easier to just stick to what works. And honestly, maybe it’s better that way. I don’t want to mess up our relationship. So, it’s just… I don’t know, I’m stuck. Please, let me know if you think I’m right or if I need to change my approach. Thanks for putting me on the spot, and I really appreciate you being here. You really help me navigate through this.

I haven’t really spoken up at the family gathering since that moment. I just felt like no one was paying attention, and it seemed like whatever I said didn’t matter much. When they started criticizing my career choices, I shut down, afraid to explain why I’m uncomfortable with how things are happening. After that, I felt completely isolated, and honestly, I wanted to leave earlier so I could get away from it all. It’s like I’m always feeling like I don’t belong there, even though I know I probably shouldn’t. I guess I just feel stuck in this pattern where I try to change things, but sometimes it ends up in disappointment instead. It’s hard to break free from those overwhelming emotions. I appreciate you noticing this, but I’m scared I’m just going to keep repeating the same thing. Could we maybe talk about something different tomorrow? That way, I won’t feel like sticking my head out. Thank you for being here. I really want to start making connections again.

Well, last weekend was pretty tense when my partner suggested going separate ways, and I felt really stuck. I kept thinking, “Why don’t they just stay together instead of separating? It’s like if I’m not there, everything will fall apart.” So, I ended up making some changes, changing how we plan things so that maybe they could still spend time together, even though I know it won’t happen. I guess I just wanted to prove that I wasn’t going to lose them because it felt safer than risking losing myself again. Honestly, it’s hard to stop feeling overwhelmed and sad at the same time. I wish I could tell them right away what’s happening, but I’m scared they’d think I’m being too much of a burden. It’s frustrating because I want to be honest about how I feel, but I just don’t know how to start the conversation. Maybe if I’ve already made all these arrangements, they’ll understand why I’m struggling. But then again, it’s easier to keep pushing through than face the possibility of losing everything. So, I guess I’m just okay with feeling this way. It’s hard to admit it until someone asks. Thanks for walking me through this. It’s reassuring to know people usually respond to questions in an honest way. I appreciate you playing along.

Yeah, this situation has really hit me hard lately. Every time I get a new project, I just feel like maybe I’m going to mess up somewhere, and then everyone will notice how useless I am. I keep thinking, “Why bother trying when I’ll always fall short? It’s better to just stay quiet and avoid making things worse.” Sometimes I just sit down and double-check everything again and again, hoping it’s okay, but I still feel like something’s wrong. It’s like I’m always putting myself on the line, but instead, I just end up disappointing myself and proving it’s all false. I don’t know why I keep pushing so hard; it feels exhausting and pointless sometimes. I’ve tried different ways to tackle it, but nothing seems to fix what feels like a never-ending cycle of disappointment. It’s like I’m stuck in this place where I’m constantly questioning whether I’m cut out for my job or if I’m just not good enough. I want to change, but I’m scared of what people might say. So, I just prefer to stay where I am—maybe even avoid the whole thing altogether. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how else to get through this. I guess I just feel like I’m failing at being someone who matters. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I can’t escape this feeling of being out of control. I’d love to tell someone about how much I’m struggling, but I’m too afraid of seeming weak or embarrassing myself. Can you ever see that inside me, even a little bit, that I’m not good enough for this? It’s like I’m trapped in this part of my life that doesn’t seem to belong to me. I’m sorry, I’m just so lost here. Please, help me figure out how to let go of this feeling and start feeling more confident in my

I don’t know, it’s just really hard. When my brother got this big business, he was all hooting and hollering about it, like he was saying I’m the loser again. Then when my mom started talking about me, she just kept going on and on, like, “What’s wrong with you? Why are you always so behind?” I ended up just sitting there, kind of shut down, even though I knew I had nothing to say. Honestly, it just made me feel worse and sad inside. It’s like no matter how much I work or try, it’s never enough. I guess I just feel like I’m falling further behind every time I step outside the comfort zone. It’s exhausting being constantly feeling like I’m invisible or weak. I’ve tried to figure out why I’m stuck here, but nothing seems to work. It’s like I’m trapped in this cycle where I feel like I’m just too small to make any difference. I’d prefer to just go home and relax instead of feeling like this constant pain. So, I just leave early and pretend nothing happened. But inside, I’m still very sorry and wondering if I ever really belong. I hope someone can see through to the root of this issue before I lose myself completely. Please let me know if there’s anything I said that doesn’t seem right. I need someone to tell me what’s really happening before it’s too late. Thank you so much for your time. I appreciate you being here. You’re really helping me navigate this. I’ll be back later. You’re lucky to be here. Thanks!

Yeah, I guess I’ve been really worried about going to that party lately. But then when I got that invite, I just couldn’t shake this feeling of being too much like myself and maybe making things worse. I keep thinking, “Why bother” when everyone seems so interested in meeting me. It’s like they’re just waiting for me to mess something up or seem too silly, and then they’ll just go away. So, instead of going, I just stayed home. I feel kind of embarrassed and sad at the same time. It’s hard to shake off the whole idea that if I’m there, I might just disappoint someone or let them down somehow. I don’t know, it just feels harder to step forward than pretend to be someone nobody wants to meet. It’s like I’m always trying to prove myself, and when I can’t, it makes me feel worse. I wonder if it’s just because of how people usually react to me? Like they’re already seeing me as the type of person they don’t want to be around anymore. It’s exhausting to try to navigate through this. I hope I don’t give in altogether, but I’m scared of what that means. It feels safer not to say anything. Please help me figure out how to feel better about going. I’d really appreciate it if you could just give me some guidance. Thanks!

Yeah, the dinner was... kind of hard for me. When my parents were talking about my sibling, I just couldn’t stop thinking about how different it must feel for them to be acknowledging my sibling’s success, when I know I’m just behind. It made me feel like maybe they’re not even looking at me or seeing how much I’ve come out. I didn’t want to say anything because I didn’t want to seem weak or give them any reason to think less of me. Afterward, I kept thinking about that moment and replaying it in my head over and over, which just made me feel worse and more alone. It’s like I’m always being judged too harshly. I guess I just feel so insignificant, like no matter what I do, it won’t match up with what others expect of me. It’s exhausting to keep pushing forward despite feeling like I’m failing. I wish there was some way to talk about this without making things worse. It’s like I’m stuck between being trapped in my own pain and facing someone who doesn’t believe in me. I’m sorry I wasn’t able to be more open about it. It feels like I’m carrying around this heavy weight that I don’t need to bear. Please let me know if I can’t do this right. I really appreciate you understanding. Thank you for being here. I love you.

Yeah, I guess I felt kind of stuck at first. When they started talking about the community group, I just kind of froze up because I was worried they’d notice I wasn’t really contributing much or maybe even didn’t understand what I was saying. I kept trying to keep things light, but then I found myself agreeing too easily when people said something interesting or interesting, which made me feel like I was being forced onto something I didn’t want to do. It’s frustrating because I want to try something new, but I’m scared of losing myself or being left out. I’ve tried to talk more, but it feels like no matter how much I say or try to connect, it never quite sticks. It’s like I’m trapped in this cycle where I’m always hoping to prove myself, but instead, I end up feeling even more isolated or like I’m failing. I’ve been thinking about asking them if maybe we could find a different activity or someone else that interests me instead, but I’m hesitant because I don’t want to seem pushy or needy. It’s like I’m afraid of starting something awkward or disappointing. So, I just stick to what I already know and hope it works out better than staying quiet. It’s frustrating because I want to be part of something that can grow and help me improve, but I just feel like I’m missing something crucial. I’m glad I didn’t go into more details right away; it just feels easier to just stay quiet. And honestly, sometimes it feels like I’m just wasting time. But I don’t know, I feel like this connection isn’t real until I step outside of myself and try something new. It’s like I’m waiting for the other shoe to drop. So, I’m sorry I haven’t been honest with you earlier. Please, let me know if there’s anything I can do differently to feel more connected.

Yeah, this week was kind of rough at work. When I started talking, everyone just seemed to drift off, and I didn’t really feel like joining in. I kept thinking maybe I’m not really cut out for this kind of interaction anyway. It feels like I’m invisible because people just don’t seem to notice where I am or what I’m saying. Sometimes I wonder if I’m being too obvious or not good enough. Honestly, I feel pretty down afterwards—like I’m just not good enough to be around anyone. It’s hard to stop replaying what happened and wondering why I always end up feeling so left out.
